matchup: Oklahoma City Thunder @ San Antonio Spurs

closing_market_snapshot.moneyline_consensus: SAS favored; best shown SAS -133 to -155 range, OKC +115 to +131 range

actual_game_script.summary: SAS controlled every quarter; OKC never reached 23 points in any quarter. Game total closed around 219 but finished 185, driven by OKC scoring collapse and poor shooting efficiency.

market_total.assessment: Market badly overestimated scoring environment. Possession count was normal/high, but shot conversion collapsed, especially OKC 36.26 eFG and 18.18% from three.

team_totals.assessment: SAS did not clear the newly shown Game 5 style 110+ TT range despite control; OKC under is the stronger continuation angle unless market heavily corrects downward.

rebound_environment_analysis.summary: Total rebounds were extremely high: OKC 59, SAS 60. This was not one-team dominance; it was miss-volume inflation. Both teams had 30+ offensive rebound rate profiles, with SAS slightly better FG OReb% at 32.73 vs OKC 31.67.
rebound_environment_analysis.key_read: Rebound overs can remain live even if scoring unders hit. Miss volume created extra chances while poor efficiency suppressed points.
player_targeting_notes: Wembanyama remains stable at 8+ boards with 14 chances and 66.7 adjusted chance pct.
Kornet was highly efficient on glass: 7 rebounds in 13.4 minutes from 15 chances; bench-big minutes spike flag required.
Fox guard rebounds are a real Game 5 watch: 10 rebounds, 15 chances, 10.9 avg rebound distance, 0 contested rebounds, indicating long-rebound capture rather than paint fight.
Chet/Hartenstein rebounds were supported by huge OKC miss environment but minutes split creates ceiling uncertainty.

OKC: Chet only 25.6 minutes and Hartenstein 17.9; combined frontcourt rebounding strong but individual minute volatility remains high.
SGA logged 31.3 minutes, 4 rebounds on 9 chances; team offensive burden did not translate to efficient scoring environment from available team-level data.
Jared McCain had 9 rebound chances but only 2 boards with 18.4 avg rebound distance, indicating long-ball rebound volatility not reliable.
SAS: Wembanyama 31 minutes, Fox 30.9, Vassell 33.0, Castle 29.9: starters remained stable in controlled win.
Kornet 13.4 minutes with 7 rebounds demands bench-big spike tag.

patch_evaluation.OKC_ABOVE_BREAK_THREE_FAILURE: ADD — OKC shot 3/23 on above-break threes, 13.04%, creating both scoring suppression and rebound inflation.
patch_evaluation.MISS_VOLUME_REBOUND_INFLATION: ADD — total rebounds 119 with final total only 185; rebound overs can decouple from game over.
patch_evaluation.SAS_SIZE_REBOUND_STABILITY: KEEP/ADD — Wembanyama plus Kornet created stable interior rebound profile.
patch_evaluation.FOX_LONG_REBOUND_CAPTURE: ADD — Fox 10 rebounds, 15 chances, zero contested boards, 10.9 avg distance.
patch_evaluation.OKC_TEAM_TOTAL_SUPPRESSION: ADD — OKC scored 82 on 99 possessions with 36.26 eFG and 20 turnovers.
patch_evaluation.SAS_TT_OVER_CAUTION: ADD — SAS controlled the game but only scored 103; side and team total should not be treated as same signal.

initial_model_bias: OKC TT Under remains strongest continuation angle if 108.5+ is available.
Full-game under remains live, but market may correct from Game 4 extreme.
SAS spread/ML remains cleaner than SAS TT over because Game 4 showed control without scoring explosion.
Rebounds: Wembanyama, Kornet minute-dependent, Fox long rebounds, and Chet/Hartenstein split-minute monitoring are priority markets.

recommended_sim_modifiers.SAS_team_total_ceiling_cap: apply unless OKC pace/turnover live-ball runouts spike

final_status: KEEP SAS side/control signal; ADD OKC offensive suppression and miss-volume rebound inflation patches; MODIFY SAS team-total treatment with ceiling caution.
